steps.title: Rearrange the inequality
steps.content:
$3x - 7y > 21 \implies -7y > -3x + 21 \implies y < \dfrac{3}{7}x - 3$.
The solution set is all points below the line $y = \frac{3}{7}x - 3$ (y-intercept $-3$, x-intercept $7$, positive slope).

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The region where $x < 0$ and $y > 0$: When $x < 0$, the boundary line gives $y = \frac{3}{7}x - 3 < -3$. Every point in this region has $y > 0 > $ (line value), so all points lie above the line. None satisfy $y < \frac{3}{7}x - 3$. ✓
  • Choice B — The region where $x > 0$ and $y < 0$: Test $(8, -1)$: $3(8) - 7(-1) = 24 + 7 = 31 > 21$. Contains solutions. ✗
  • Choice C — The region where $x > 0$ and $y > 0$: Test $(100, 1)$: $300 - 7 = 293 > 21$. Contains solutions. ✗
  • Choice D — The $x$-axis: On the x-axis $y = 0$, the inequality becomes $3x > 21 \implies x > 7$. Points like $(8, 0)$ lie on the x-axis and satisfy it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
